fix: Switched to public CSV download: https://urlhaus.abuse.ch/downloads/csv_recent/ and added _parse_urlhaus_csv()

error_message: Cache hits for MALICIOUS/SUSPICIOUS IOCs never triggered alerts — zero alerts fired without VT key
root_cause: _lookup_worker emitted SocketIO events for MALICIOUS cache hits but never called alert_engine.evaluate()
fix: Added alert evaluation block in _lookup_worker for MALICIOUS/SUSPICIOUS threats, using effective_score derived from vt_score or synthetic fallback

error_message: SplunkIngester ignored scheme, token, earliest_time, latest_time from Config
root_cause: Old constructor took positional args and hardcoded earliest_time='rt-5m', latest_time='rt', never read Config.SPLUNK_SCHEME or Config.SPLUNK_TOKEN
fix: Rewrote to no-args constructor that reads all settings from Config via _build_connect_kwargs()

error_message: Attack simulation panel: only 1 alert fires per IOC — subsequent clicks produce no alert
root_cause: has_recent_alert() used a 1-hour time-based cooldown. After the first injection fired an alert, all subsequent injections of the same IOC were blocked for 60 minutes.
fix: 1) Changed has_recent_alert to only block if an open status='NEW' alert exists (no time window). 2) In api_test_inject, added UPDATE to auto-acknowledge any open NEW alerts for that IOC before seeding, so each button press always fires fresh. 3) Added POST /api/test/reset endpoint and Reset Alerts button in dashboard.

error_message: Browser shows 'Not Found / The requested URL was not found on the server' on http://127.0.0.1:5000/ even though run.py started cleanly and url_map contains '/'
root_cause: Multiple stale `python run.py` processes from previous sessions were still LISTENING on port 5000. On Windows, several sockets can bind to the same port via SO_REUSEADDR; incoming requests get distributed to whichever PID accepts first, and a stale server with an older blueprint (or no blueprint at all) returns 404 while the new server is shadowed.
fix: 1) `netstat -ano | grep ':5000.*LISTENING'` to find all PIDs on the port. 2) `taskkill //F //PID <pid>` for every stale PID. 3) Restart `python run.py`. After cleanup the new server responds 200 on / and /api/health.
